title: Quiz: Architecture Design for Trainability
description: Test your understanding of circuit architecture design principles

questions.question: How should circuit depth scale with qubit count to avoid barren plateaus?
questions.explanation: To avoid expressibility-induced barren plateaus, circuit depth should scale logarithmically with qubit count, L ~ O(log n).

questions.question: Which entanglement pattern is most likely to cause severe barren plateaus?
questions.explanation: All-to-all entanglement creates maximum entanglement growth, leading to the most severe barren plateaus.

questions.question: What is a hierarchical ansatz?

options.text: A circuit that only uses single-qubit gates
A circuit that processes information at multiple scales/levels
A circuit with all parameters equal

questions.explanation: Hierarchical ansätze process information at multiple scales (like tree structures), which helps avoid global barren plateaus.

questions.question: Higher expressibility always leads to better learning outcomes.
questions.explanation: There's a trade-off: highly expressive circuits often have barren plateaus, making training impossible despite theoretical expressibility.

questions.question: Why does sparse entanglement help with trainability?

options.text: It limits entanglement growth, reducing gradient suppression

questions.explanation: Sparse entanglement (e.g., alternating pairs) limits how quickly entanglement builds up, keeping gradient variance larger.

questions.question: For n=8 qubits, what is a reasonable maximum depth if targeting gradient variance > 10^{-4}?
questions.explanation: For 8 qubits, very shallow circuits (1-2 layers) are needed to maintain gradient variance above 10^{-4} with typical architectures.

questions.question: What characterizes a problem-specific ansatz?

options.text: It incorporates structure from the problem being solved
It has the most parameters possible
It is randomly generated for each problem

questions.explanation: Problem-specific ansätze (like QAOA or VQE) incorporate problem structure, which can improve trainability over generic hardware-efficient designs.

questions.question: Which design choices improve trainability? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Limiting depth (a), sparse entanglement (b), and hierarchical structure (d) all improve trainability. Maximizing expressibility (c) typically worsens barren plateaus.
